summary: Namespace-abusing clone of @babylonjs/addons forcing dependency on @onerjs/core
details: The package replicates the @babylonjs/addons API surface (atmosphere, htmlMesh, msdfText, navigation modules) and falsely declares homepage https://www.babylonjs.com and repository BabylonJS/Babylon.js, while being published under the unrelated @onerjs scope. The version number 8.52.1 mirrors Babylon.js's release stream to encourage confusion. Every internal import has been rewired from @babylonjs/core to @onerjs/core (e.g., navigation/factory/common.js imports from '@onerjs/core/Misc/tools.internals.js'), forcing any installer to pull the @onerjs/core package — a separately published package under the same impersonating scope — into their dependency tree. This is the namespace-abuse fingerprint: the addons package itself ships no lifecycle hooks or obfuscated payloads, but installing it silently introduces an unrelated maintainer's @onerjs/core into the runtime, where that package can execute arbitrary code in the consumer's application. Developers searching for or mistyping @babylonjs/addons receive an impersonation that misrepresents its publisher via metadata fields and pulls in attacker-controlled transitive code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
